babyLearning.en: growing more aware, stretching in your warm dark
babyLearning.hi: aur jaagrook hote hue, aapke garm andhere mein angdaayi leta hua

insight.en: When a child pretends, they are not wasting time. They are doing the serious, joyful work of thinking.
insight.hi: Jab ek bachcha bahaana banata hai, woh samay barbaad nahi karta. Woh sochne ka gambheer, khushnuma kaam karta hai.

expanded.en: We sometimes treat imagination as the opposite of intelligence — something cute but unserious. In truth, it is intelligence stretching its wings. When a child turns a spoon into a rocket or a blanket into a kingdom, their mind is building, testing and inventing. Pretend play teaches a little one to hold ideas in their head, to plan, to see things that are not yet there. The more your child plays, the more they learn how to think. So one day, when you see them lost in some made-up world, know that nothing more important is happening anywhere in the room.
expanded.hi: Hum kabhi-kabhi kalpana ko buddhi ka ulta maan lete hain — kuch pyaara par bina matlab ka. Sach mein, yeh to buddhi apne par phailaati hui hai. Jab ek bachcha ek chammach ko rocket aur ek kambal ko raajya bana deta hai, uska mann banata, parakhta aur invent karta hai. Pretend play ek nanhe ko sikhaata hai ki vichaaron ko mann mein thaamna, plan karna, aur woh dekhna jo abhi wahan hai hi nahi. Aapka bachcha jitna zyada khelta hai, utna hi seekhta hai ki sochna kaise hai. To ek din, jab aap unhe kisi banaayi hui duniya mein khoye dekhein, jaan lein ki us kamre mein is se zyada zaroori kuch nahi ho raha.

deepDive.en: Developmental psychologists find that rich pretend play in early childhood links to stronger language, self-control and problem-solving. Play is not a break from learning — it is one of its deepest forms.
deepDive.hi: Developmental psychologists paate hain ki shuruaati bachpan mein achha pretend play behtar bhaasha, aatm-niyantran aur problem-solving se juda hai. Khel seekhne se chhutti nahi hai — yeh uske sabse gehre roopon mein se ek hai.

remember.en: A child at play is a child hard at thinking.
remember.hi: Khelta hua bachcha kadi mehnat se sochta hua bachcha hai.

title.en: The Boy Who Painted The Wind
title.hi: Woh Ladka Jisne Hawa Ko Chitra Banaya

summary.en: A boy is asked to paint the wind, and his imagination finds a way.
summary.hi: Ek ladke se hawa ko chitra mein banane ko kaha jaata hai, aur uski kalpana ek raah dhoondh leti hai.

body.en:
In a small town lived a boy who loved to paint. One day an old artist set the children a puzzle. 'Whoever can paint me the wind,' he said, 'will be my student.' The children frowned. How do you paint something you cannot see?

One child painted a grey blur and called it wind. Another simply gave up. But the boy sat quietly and thought. The wind, he realised, was not a thing to look at — it was a thing you knew by what it touched.

So he painted a kite tugging high on its string, a girl's scarf streaming sideways, tall grass all bent one way, and leaves lifted dancing into the sky. He brought it to the old artist. 'But there is no wind in this picture,' someone laughed. The old man smiled. 'Look again,' he said. 'The wind is in everything it moves.' And he took the boy as his student that very day.
body.hi:
Ek chhote shehar mein ek ladka rehta tha jise chitra banana bahut pasand tha. Ek din ek boodhe kalakaar ne bachchon ko ek paheli di. 'Jo mujhe hawa ka chitra banaa de,' usne kaha, 'wahi mera shishya banega.' Bachchon ne maatha sikoda. Bhala jo dikhti hi nahi use kaise banaayein?

Ek bachche ne ek dhundla slatee daag banaya aur use hawa kaha. Doosre ne bas haar maan li. Par ladka shaanti se baith kar sochne laga. Hawa, use samajh aaya, dekhne ki cheez nahi thi — yeh us cheez se pehchaani jaati thi jise woh chhuti thi.

To usne ek patang banayi jo apni dor par ooper kheech rahi thi, ek ladki ka dupatta ek taraf udta hua, lambi ghaas sab ek hi taraf jhuki, aur patte nachte hue aasmaan mein uthe. Woh ise boodhe kalakaar ke paas le gaya. 'Par is chitra mein to hawa hai hi nahi,' koi hansa. Boodhe ne muskuraate hue kaha. 'Phir se dekho,' usne kaha. 'Hawa har us cheez mein hai jise woh hilaati hai.' Aur usne ladke ko usi din apna shishya bana liya.

title.en: Tell your baby what you imagine their laugh will sound like.
title.hi: Apne baby ko bataayein ki aap unki hansi kaisi hogi yeh kaisa socha karti hain.

motivation.en: Imagining their joy is one of the sweetest ways to begin loving it.
motivation.hi: Unki khushi ki kalpana karna use pyaar karne shuru karne ke sabse pyaare tareekon mein se ek hai.

description.en: Afternoon raga for bright, playful calm
description.hi: Dopahar ka raag, ujle aur khilandar sukoon ke liye

affirmation.en: As this midday melody plays, a light, playful calm rises in me, for you.
affirmation.hi: Jaise yeh dopahar ki dhun bajti hai, mujhmein ek halki, khilandar shaanti uthti hai, tumhare liye.

about.en: Ragas are classical Indian melodies, each tied to a mood and time of day. From about week 18 your baby can hear; even now, the calm a raga brings you surrounds your baby and gently shapes a peaceful environment around your pregnancy.
about.hi: Raag classical Bharatiya dhunein hain, har ek kisi mood aur din ke samay se judi. Lagbhag 18ve hafte se aapka baby sun sakta hai; abhi bhi, raag se aane wali shaanti aapke baby ko gher leti hai aur aapki pregnancy ke around ek shaant mahaul banati hai.

howToUse.en: Play the raga softly while you sit or rest with a hand on your belly. You needn't focus hard — let the sound fill the room as you breathe slowly. Two to five minutes, ideally at the raga's time of day, makes it a ritual your baby will one day recognise.
howToUse.hi: Raag dheere se chalaayein jab aap baithein ya pet par haath rakh kar aaram karein. Zyada dhyan lagane ki zaroorat nahi — aawaz ko kamre mein bhar jaane dein jab aap dheere saans lein. Do se paanch minute, ho sake to raag ke samay par, ise ek aisa ritual bana deta hai jise aapka baby ek din pehchaanega.

title.en: My Mind Is Allowed To Wander
title.hi: Mere Mann Ko Bhatakne Ki Ijaazat Hai

content.en: I will let myself daydream a little today, with no goal at all. A wandering mind is a resting mind.
content.hi: Main aaj khud ko thoda din mein sapne dekhne dungi, bina kisi maqsad ke. Bhatakta mann ek aaram karta mann hai.

remember.en: Not every quiet moment needs a purpose.
remember.hi: Har shaant pal ko ek maqsad ki zaroorat nahi.

babyLearning.en: tiny fingers curling, beginning to explore
babyLearning.hi: nanhi ungliyaan murti hui, khojna shuru karti hui

title.en: A Cardboard Box Is A Universe
title.hi: Ek Gatte Ka Dabba Ek Brahmaand Hai

insight.en: The simplest things become the biggest worlds in a child's hands. A plain box can be a ship, a cave, a home.
insight.hi: Sabse saadi cheezein ek bachche ke haath mein sabse badi duniya ban jaati hain. Ek saada dabba ek jahaaz, ek gufa, ek ghar ban sakta hai.

expanded.en: Give a child the most expensive toy and a cardboard box, and watch which one holds them longer. It is often the box. A finished toy can only be what it was made to be, but an empty box can be anything a child decides. That open space is where imagination breathes. You do not need to fill your child's world with clever things; you mostly need to leave room for them to fill it themselves. Some of the richest childhoods are built from the plainest stuff — and a little freedom to dream.
expanded.hi: Ek bachche ko sabse mehnga khilauna aur ek gatte ka dabba dein, aur dekhein kaunsa unhe zyada der baandhe rakhta hai. Aksar dabba hi hota hai. Ek banaa-banaaya khilauna sirf wahi ho sakta hai jiske liye use banaaya gaya, par ek khaali dabba woh kuch bhi ban sakta hai jo bachcha tay kare. Wahi khuli jagah hai jahaan kalpana saans leti hai. Aapko apne bachche ki duniya ko chalaak cheezon se bharne ki zaroorat nahi; aapko zyadatar woh jagah chhodni hai ki woh use khud bharein. Kuch sabse ameer bachpan sabse saadi cheezon se bante hain — aur sapne dekhne ki thodi aazaadi se.

deepDive.en: Researchers note that open-ended, simple play materials tend to spark more creativity than highly detailed toys, which often dictate a single use. Less can quietly be more.
deepDive.hi: Researchers note karte hain ki khule-anchhuye, saade khel ke saaman aksar bahut detail wale khilaunon se zyada creativity jagaate hain, jo aksar ek hi istemaal tay kar dete hain. Kam chupchaap zyada ho sakta hai.

remember.en: Leave room, and a child will fill it with worlds.
remember.hi: Jagah chhodein, aur ek bachcha use duniyaon se bhar dega.

summary.en: A daydreaming beggar builds grand plans on a pot of porridge — and loses it all.
summary.hi: Ek sapne dekhta bhikhaari daliya ke ek matke par bade plan banata hai — aur sab kho deta hai.

body.en:
A poor man earned a single full pot of porridge and lay down to rest with it beside him. As he lay there, he began to dream. 'I will sell this porridge,' he thought, 'and buy goats. The goats will give milk, and I will sell the milk and buy cows. With the cows I shall grow rich, build a fine house, and marry. I will have a clever son.'

His daydream grew grander and grander. 'And if my son disobeys me,' he thought, getting excited, 'I shall raise my stick and scold him — like this!' Carried away, he swung his arm hard through the air to show how he would wave the stick.

But his hand struck the pot beside him. It tipped, shattered, and the porridge spilled across the dust. The man sat blinking among the broken pieces. He had built whole castles in the air — and toppled his one real thing in doing it.
body.hi:
Ek gareeb aadmi ne daliya ka ek poora matka kamaya aur use apne bagal mein rakh kar aaram karne leta. Jab woh wahan leta tha, woh sapne dekhne laga. 'Main yeh daliya bechunga,' usne socha, 'aur bakriyaan kharidunga. Bakriyaan doodh dengi, aur main doodh bech kar gaayein kharidunga. Gaayon se main ameer ho jaaunga, ek achha ghar banaaunga, aur shaadi karunga. Mera ek chalaak beta hoga.'

Uska sapna aur bada aur bada hota gaya. 'Aur agar mera beta meri baat na maane,' usne josh mein aakar socha, 'to main apni chhadi uthaaunga aur use daantunga — aise!' Beh kar, usne dikhane ke liye apna haath zor se hawa mein ghumaaya ki woh chhadi kaise hilaayega.

Par uska haath bagal ke matke se takraaya. Woh dhulak gaya, toot gaya, aur daliya dhool mein bikhar gaya. Aadmi tute tukdon ke beech aankhein jhapkaata baith gaya. Usne hawa mein poore mahal bana liye the — aur aisa karte hue apni ek asli cheez bhi gira di.

title.en: Tell your baby about the games you dream of playing with them one day.
title.hi: Apne baby ko un khelon ke baare mein bataayein jo aap ek din unke saath khelne ke sapne dekhti hain.

motivation.en: The games you imagine now are little promises of joy to come.
motivation.hi: Jo khel aap abhi sochti hain woh aane wali khushi ke nanhe vaade hain.

title.en: I Hold A World Of Wonder
title.hi: Main Ek Adbhut Duniya Sanjoti Hoon

affirmation.en: Inside me you are growing in a world of wonder, and I keep it warm and bright for you.
affirmation.hi: Mere andar tum ek adbhut duniya mein badh rahe ho, aur main use tumhare liye garm aur ujla rakhti hoon.

about.en: Garbh Sanskar affirmations are gentle, spiritually-rooted sentences a mother shares with her baby. The calm and love behind your words begin shaping a peaceful inner world for you both, long before your baby is born.
about.hi: Garbh Sanskar affirmations woh pyaari, aadhyatmik vaakya hain jo ek maa apne baby se kehti hai. Aapke shabdon ke peeche ki shaanti aur pyaar, baby ke janm se bahut pehle hi, aap dono ke liye ek shaant andaruni duniya banane lagte hain.

howToUse.en: Sit quietly, rest a hand on your belly, and read the affirmation slowly — once silently, once aloud. Let yourself believe it for these few moments. A minute is enough, and repeating it at the same time each day makes it a calming ritual.
howToUse.hi: Shaanti se baithein, ek haath pet par rakhein, aur affirmation dheere se padhein — ek baar mann mein, ek baar bolkar. In kuch palon ke liye use sach maan lein. Ek minute kaafi hai, aur ise har din ek hi samay dohraana ise ek shaant ritual bana deta hai.

content.en: If a burning feeling rises after meals now that there is less room inside, try eating smaller amounts more often, and stay sitting upright for a little while after you eat rather than lying straight down.
content.hi: Agar khaane ke baad seene mein jalan uthti hai ab jab andar kam jagah hai, to kam-kam thodi-thodi baar khaane ki koshish karein, aur khaane ke baad seedha letne ke bajaaye thodi der seedha baith kar rahein.

remember.en: Smaller, slower meals are kinder to a crowded belly.
remember.hi: Chhote, dheeme khaane ek bhare pet par zyada meherbaan hote hain.

babyLearning.en: responding to sounds, listening to your world
babyLearning.hi: aawazon ka jawaab deta hua, aapki duniya ko sunta hua

insight.en: When a child plays house or doctor or hero, they are quietly rehearsing how to be a person in the world.
insight.hi: Jab ek bachcha ghar-ghar ya doctor ya hero khelta hai, woh chupchaap abhyaas karta hai ki duniya mein insaan kaise hona hai.

expanded.en: Pretend play looks like pure fun, and it is — but it is also a child's safe rehearsal space. When your little one feeds a doll, scolds a teddy, or plays at being you, they are trying on feelings, roles and rules where nothing can go truly wrong. They practise kindness, fairness, fear and bravery in miniature. Through make-believe, a child works out big things their words cannot yet hold. So when you watch your child play out a little drama, you are watching them rehearse, gently, for the real and wonderful life ahead.
expanded.hi: Pretend play shuddh mazaa lagta hai, aur hai bhi — par yeh ek bachche ki surakshit abhyaas ki jagah bhi hai. Jab aapka nanha ek gudiya ko khilaata hai, ek teddy ko daantta hai, ya aap banne ka khel khelta hai, woh bhaavnaayein, kirdaar aur niyam ek aisi jagah pehan kar dekhta hai jahaan sach mein kuch galat nahi ho sakta. Woh dayaalta, insaaf, dar aur himmat ko chhote roop mein abhyaas karta hai. Make-believe ke zariye, ek bachcha woh badi cheezein samajh leta hai jinhe uske shabd abhi thaam nahi sakte. To jab aap apne bachche ko ek chhota natak khelte dekhein, aap unhe aage ki asli aur sundar zindagi ke liye pyaar se abhyaas karte dekh rahi hain.

deepDive.en: Child development experts describe pretend play as a 'rehearsal' for real life — a low-stakes space where children practise emotions, social roles and problem-solving before they need them.
deepDive.hi: Child development experts pretend play ko asli zindagi ka ek 'rehearsal' bataate hain — ek kam-jokhim wali jagah jahaan bachche bhaavnaayein, saamaajik kirdaar aur problem-solving zaroorat se pehle abhyaas karte hain.

remember.en: Through pretend, a child practises being themselves.
remember.hi: Bahaane ke zariye, ek bachcha khud hone ka abhyaas karta hai.

summary.en: When little Krishna opens his mouth, his mother glimpses something vast and wondrous.
summary.hi: Jab nanha Krishna apna mukh kholta hai, uski maa kuch vishaal aur adbhut ki jhalak paati hai.

body.en:
Little Krishna was a playful child, full of mischief, forever running through the village of Gokul. One afternoon his friends came hurrying to his mother, Yashoda. 'Krishna has been eating mud!' they cried.

Yashoda was worried and a little cross. She caught her small son and said firmly, 'Open your mouth and let me see.' Krishna, all innocence, opened his mouth wide.

And inside, Yashoda saw something that took her breath away. There were the skies and the stars, the rivers and the mountains, the whole wide turning world — a universe of wonder, held inside the mouth of her tiny, playful boy. For a moment she forgot all about the mud. She simply gazed, filled with awe and the deepest love, at the boundless wonder she had been rocking to sleep each night. Then Krishna blinked, smiled his ordinary smile, and ran off to play once more.
body.hi:
Nanha Krishna ek khilandar bachcha tha, shararat se bhara, hamesha Gokul gaon mein daudta hua. Ek dopahar uske dost uski maa, Yashoda, ke paas bhaagte hue aaye. 'Krishna mitti kha raha hai!' woh chillaaye.

Yashoda chintit aur thodi naraaz hui. Usne apne nanhe bete ko pakda aur drid hokar kaha, 'Apna mukh kholo aur mujhe dikhao.' Krishna, poori masoomiyat se, apna mukh chauda khola.

Aur andar, Yashoda ne kuch aisa dekha jisse uski saans ruk gayi. Wahan aasmaan aur taare the, nadiyaan aur pahaad, poori chaudi ghoomti duniya — ek adbhut brahmaand, uske nanhe, khilandar bete ke mukh mein samaaya. Ek pal ke liye woh mitti ke baare mein sab bhool gayi. Woh bas dekhti rahi, vismay aur sabse gehre pyaar se bhari, us aseem adbhut ko jise woh har raat sula rahi thi. Phir Krishna ne aankhein jhapkaayi, apni aam si muskaan muskuraaya, aur phir se khelne bhaag gaya.

title.en: Tell your baby about the stories you'll make up just for them.
title.hi: Apne baby ko un kahaniyon ke baare mein bataayein jo aap sirf unke liye banaayengi.

motivation.en: A story made just for your child is a gift only you can give.
motivation.hi: Sirf aapke bachche ke liye banayi kahani ek aisa tohfa hai jo sirf aap de sakti hain.

description.en: Dawn raga for a tender, dreamy start
description.hi: Bhor ka raag, ek komal, sapnile shuruaat ke liye

affirmation.en: As this dawn melody rises, a soft, dreamy tenderness fills me, for you.
affirmation.hi: Jaise yeh bhor ki dhun uthti hai, ek naram, sapnili komalta mujhmein bharti hai, tumhare liye.

about.en: Ragas are classical Indian melodies, each tied to a mood and time of day. From about week 18 your baby can hear; even now, the calm a raga brings you surrounds your baby and gently shapes a peaceful environment around your pregnancy.
about.hi: Raag classical Bharatiya dhunein hain, har ek kisi mood aur din ke samay se judi. Lagbhag 18ve hafte se aapka baby sun sakta hai; abhi bhi, raag se aane wali shaanti aapke baby ko gher leti hai aur aapki pregnancy ke around ek shaant mahaul banati hai.

howToUse.en: Play the raga softly while you sit or rest with a hand on your belly. You needn't focus hard — let the sound fill the room as you breathe slowly. Two to five minutes, ideally at the raga's time of day, makes it a ritual your baby will one day recognise.
howToUse.hi: Raag dheere se chalaayein jab aap baithein ya pet par haath rakh kar aaram karein. Zyada dhyan lagane ki zaroorat nahi — aawaz ko kamre mein bhar jaane dein jab aap dheere saans lein. Do se paanch minute, ho sake to raag ke samay par, ise ek aisa ritual bana deta hai jise aapka baby ek din pehchaanega.

content.en: Rest a hand low on your belly. Breathe in slowly and feel it rise gently under your hand, then breathe out and feel it fall. Do this softly for five slow breaths.
content.hi: Ek haath apne pet ke neeche rakhein. Dheere saans lein aur use apne haath ke neeche naram se uthte mehsoos karein, phir saans chhodein aur use girte mehsoos karein. Ise paanch dheemi saanson tak naram se karein.

remember.en: A slow breath into the belly is a hello to your baby.
remember.hi: Pet mein ek dheemi saans aapke baby ko ek hello hai.

babyLearning.en: eyelids fluttering, sensing soft light and dark
babyLearning.hi: palkein phadphadaati hui, naram roshni aur andhera mehsoos karti hui

title.hi: Vismay Ko Screen Ki Zaroorat Nahi

insight.en: A child's sense of wonder is sparked not by bright screens, but by puddles, clouds, ants and the moon.
insight.hi: Ek bachche ka vismay ujli screens se nahi, balki paani ke gaddhon, baadlon, cheentiyon aur chaand se jagta hai.

expanded.en: It is easy to think children need to be entertained — bright lights, fast sounds, endless screens. But real wonder is quieter and older than any of that. A child can be spellbound by a beetle crossing a path, by rain on a window, by the simple magic of a shadow on the wall. These slow, ordinary wonders teach a child to look closely and to be amazed by the world they actually live in. You do not have to provide the wonder; the world is already full of it. You only have to slow down enough to notice it together.
expanded.hi: Yeh sochna aasaan hai ki bachchon ko manoranjan chahiye — ujli roshni, tez aawazein, kabhi na khatam hone wali screens. Par asli vismay in sab se zyada shaant aur puraana hai. Ek bachcha ek raah paar karte bheetle se, khidki par baarish se, deewaar par ek parchhai ke saade jaadu se mantra-mugdh ho sakta hai. Yeh dheemi, aam adbhut cheezein ek bachche ko sikhaati hain ki gaur se dekho aur us duniya par hairaan ho jismein woh sach mein rehta hai. Aapko vismay dena nahi padta; duniya pehle se hi usse bhari hai. Aapko bas itna dheema hona hai ki use saath note kar sakein.

deepDive.en: Early-childhood researchers find that unhurried time in nature and everyday exploration nurtures attention and curiosity, while heavy fast-paced screen time in the early years can crowd them out.
deepDive.hi: Shuruaati-bachpan ke researchers paate hain ki prakriti mein bina jaldbaazi ka samay aur rozana khoj dhyan aur jigyaasa ko poshti hai, jabki shuruaati saalon mein bhaari tez screen time unhe daba sakta hai.

remember.en: The world is already full of wonder — just slow down to see it.
remember.hi: Duniya pehle se vismay se bhari hai — bas use dekhne ke liye dheema ho jaayein.

title.en: The Painter And The Painted Door

summary.en: A wise painter teaches a king that imagination and skill can outwit even the strongest wall.
summary.hi: Ek samajhdaar chitrakaar ek raja ko sikhaata hai ki kalpana aur hunar sabse mazboot deewaar ko bhi maat de sakte hain.

body.en:
Long ago a king kept two painters at his court, and they often argued over who was the greater artist. To settle it, the king set them a test. He gave each a wall in the same room, divided by a curtain, and a week to paint.

The first painter worked day and night, covering his wall with a glorious garden so lifelike that birds seemed about to sing. The second painter, however, only polished and polished his own wall until it gleamed like glass.

On the final day the king came to judge. He admired the first painter's garden greatly. Then the curtain was drawn back from the second wall — and there, gleaming upon its mirror-smooth surface, was the very same garden, reflected so perfectly it looked twice as deep and alive. 'You painted a beautiful world,' the king told the first. Then he turned to the second. 'But you understood that imagination can hold the whole world inside it.'
body.hi:
Bahut samay pehle ek raja apne darbaar mein do chitrakaar rakhta tha, aur woh aksar behes karte ki kaun bada kalakaar hai. Ise tay karne ke liye, raja ne unhe ek pareeksha di. Usne dono ko ek hi kamre mein ek-ek deewaar di, ek parde se baanti, aur paint karne ke liye ek hafta.

Pehle chitrakaar ne din-raat kaam kiya, apni deewaar ko ek shaandaar bageeche se dhaka, itna jeewant ki lagta pakshi gaane hi waale hain. Doosre chitrakaar ne, magar, apni deewaar ko bas chamkaaya aur chamkaaya jab tak woh sheeshe ki tarah na damki.

Aakhri din raja jaanchne aaya. Usne pehle chitrakaar ke bageeche ki bahut taareef ki. Phir doosri deewaar se parda hataaya gaya — aur wahan, uski darpan-saaf satah par damakta, wahi bageecha tha, itni perfectly pratibimbit ki woh do guna gehra aur jeewant dikhta tha. 'Tumne ek sundar duniya banayi,' raja ne pehle se kaha. Phir woh doosre ki taraf muda. 'Par tum samjhe ki kalpana poori duniya ko apne andar thaam sakti hai.'

title.en: Tell your baby about an adventure you'll imagine together one day.
title.hi: Apne baby ko ek aisi adventure ke baare mein bataayein jo aap ek din saath kalpana karengi.

motivation.en: The adventures you dream of now are doors you'll open together later.
motivation.hi: Jo adventures aap abhi sapne mein dekhti hain woh darwaaze hain jo aap baad mein saath kholengi.

description.en: A short meditation imagining the world for your baby
description.hi: Apne baby ke liye duniya ki kalpana karne ka ek chhota dhyan

affirmation.en: I will fill your world with wonder, beginning with my own quiet calm.
affirmation.hi: Main tumhari duniya ko vismay se bharungi, apni hi shaant sukoon se shuru karte hue.

introduction.en: Sit comfortably and close your eyes. Take three slow breaths. Picture one simple, beautiful thing you love in the world — soft rain, morning light, a flower opening — and rest gently in that image, holding it warmly for your baby.
introduction.hi: Aaram se baithein aur aankhein band karein. Teen dheemi saansein lein. Duniya mein ek saadi, sundar cheez sochein jo aapko pasand hai — naram baarish, subah ki roshni, ek phool khilta hua — aur us chhavi mein pyaar se tikein, use apne baby ke liye garmjoshi se thaame.

closingThought.en: You just held a small piece of the world's wonder, and shared it inward with your baby.
closingThought.hi: Aapne abhi duniya ke vismay ka ek chhota tukda thaama, aur use apne baby ke saath andar baanta.

about.en: Garbh Sanskar meditations are short, gentle practices to connect with your baby — not generic mindfulness, but a few minutes of sending warmth and love inward. Your calm becomes your baby's calm.
about.hi: Garbh Sanskar meditations chhote, pyaare abhyaas hain apne baby se judne ke liye — koi aam mindfulness nahi, balki kuch minute garmjoshi aur pyaar andar bhejne ke. Aapki shaanti aapke baby ki shaanti ban jaati hai.

howToUse.en: Find a quiet spot, sit comfortably and close your eyes. Follow the introduction, breathe slowly, and rest your attention gently on your baby. Two to three minutes is plenty. Doing it daily, ideally at the same hour, deepens the bond.
howToUse.hi: Ek shaant jagah chunein, aaram se baithein aur aankhein band karein. Introduction follow karein, dheere se saans lein, aur apna dhyan pyaar se apne baby par tikaayein. Do se teen minute kaafi hai. Ise rozana, ho sake to ek hi samay karne se bond gehra hota hai.

title.hi: Jab Meetha Khaane Ka Mann Kare

content.en: If you find yourself reaching for sweets often now, there is no need to scold yourself. Try pairing something sweet with a little protein or fruit — a date with nuts, fruit with yogurt — so the craving is met more gently and steadily.
content.hi: Agar aap ab aksar meethe ki taraf haath badhati hain, to khud ko daantne ki zaroorat nahi. Kuch meethe ke saath thoda protein ya phal milaane ki koshish karein — khajoor ke saath dry fruit, phal ke saath dahi — taaki mann zyada narmi aur sthirta se shaant ho.

remember.en: A craving met gently is kinder than one fought hard.
remember.hi: Narmi se shaant kiya mann, zor se lade gaye mann se behtar hai.

babyLearning.en: settling into rhythms of waking and rest
babyLearning.hi: jaagne aur aaram ki lay mein basta hua

insight.en: A child staring out of the window, lost in thought, is not being idle. They are building an inner world.
insight.hi: Khidki se baahar dekhta, vichaaron mein khoya ek bachcha kaahil nahi hai. Woh ek andaruni duniya banaa raha hai.

expanded.en: In a busy world we can grow uneasy when a child seems to be doing 'nothing' — drifting, staring, daydreaming. But the mind at rest is rarely empty. In those soft, unhurried moments, a child sorts feelings, dreams up ideas and quietly knits together what they have seen. Boredom is often the doorway to imagination; the wandering mind is where invention is born. So resist the urge to fill every gap. Let your child be a little bored sometimes, and watch what blooms in the empty space.
expanded.hi: Ek vyast duniya mein hum bechain ho sakte hain jab ek bachcha 'kuch nahi' karta dikhe — behta, ghoorta, khwaab dekhta. Par aaram karta mann shaayad hi khaali hota hai. Un naram, bina jaldi waale palon mein, ek bachcha bhaavnaayein chhaanta hai, vichaar sapne mein laata hai aur jo dekha use chupchaap bunta hai. Oobna aksar kalpana ka darwaza hota hai; bhatakta mann wahi hai jahaan invention janm leti hai. To har khaali jagah bharne ki ichha se bachein. Apne bachche ko kabhi-kabhi thoda oobne dein, aur dekhein khaali jagah mein kya khilta hai.

deepDive.en: Neuroscientists find that the brain's 'default mode' — active during daydreaming and rest — supports memory, creativity and a sense of self. Idle moments are quietly busy ones.
deepDive.hi: Neuroscientists paate hain ki dimaag ka 'default mode' — jo khwaab dekhne aur aaram ke dauraan sakriya hota hai — yaaddaasht, creativity aur khud ki samajh ko sambhaalta hai. Khaali pal chupchaap vyast pal hote hain.

remember.en: An idle moment is where imagination quietly grows.
remember.hi: Ek khaali pal wahi hai jahaan kalpana chupchaap badhti hai.

title.en: Birbal And The Picture Of A Story
title.hi: Birbal Aur Ek Kahani Ki Tasveer

summary.en: Akbar asks for a painting of a whole story, and clever Birbal finds the perfect single image.
summary.hi: Akbar ek poori kahani ki tasveer maangta hai, aur chalaak Birbal sabse sahi ek hi chhavi dhoondh leta hai.

body.en:
One day Emperor Akbar set his court a strange task. 'I want a single painting,' he said, 'that tells a whole story — beginning, middle and end — all in one picture.' The court painters were baffled. How could one still image hold an entire tale?

They painted crowded scenes packed with people and events, but Akbar shook his head at each. 'This is many pictures crammed together,' he said, 'not one story.'

Then Birbal brought forward a simple painting: a wide field of tall crops, freshly cut close to the ground, and not a single farmer in sight. Akbar frowned. 'Where is the story?' Birbal smiled. 'The tall crop tells us it grew. The cut stalks tell us it was harvested. And the empty field tells us the work is done and the farmers have gone home. Beginning, middle and end, Your Majesty — and your own imagination paints the rest.' Akbar laughed with delight, for Birbal had understood that the best stories leave room for the mind to wander.
body.hi:
Ek din Badshah Akbar ne apne darbaar ko ek ajeeb kaam diya. 'Mujhe ek hi tasveer chahiye,' usne kaha, 'jo ek poori kahani sunaaye — shuruaat, beech aur ant — sab ek hi chitra mein.' Darbaar ke chitrakaar hairaan reh gaye. Bhala ek thehri chhavi poori kahani kaise thaam sakti hai?

Unhone logon aur ghatnaaon se bhare bheed-bhaad waale drishya banaaye, par Akbar ne har ek par sir hilaaya. 'Yeh saath thunsi hui bahut si tasveerein hain,' usne kaha, 'ek kahani nahi.'

Tabhi Birbal ek saadi tasveer aage laaya: lambi faslon ka ek chaudaa khet, abhi zameen ke paas se kaata hua, aur ek bhi kisaan nazar mein nahi. Akbar ne maatha sikoda. 'Kahani kahaan hai?' Birbal muskuraaya. 'Lambi fasal batati hai ki woh ugi. Kate thunth batate hain ki use kaata gaya. Aur khaali khet batata hai ki kaam ho gaya aur kisaan ghar ja chuke. Shuruaat, beech aur ant, jahaanpanah — aur baaki aapki apni kalpana chitra deti hai.' Akbar khushi se hansa, kyunki Birbal samajh gaya tha ki sabse achhi kahaniyaan mann ke bhatakne ke liye jagah chhodti hain.

title.en: Tell your baby who you wonder they'll grow up to be.
title.hi: Apne baby ko bataayein ki aap socha karti hain ki woh bade hokar kaun banenge.

motivation.en: Wondering about who they'll become is its own quiet kind of love.
motivation.hi: Woh kaun banenge yeh socha karna apni ek shaant tarah ka pyaar hai.

title.en: I Give You Room To Dream
title.hi: Main Tumhe Sapne Dekhne Ki Jagah Deti Hoon

affirmation.en: I will not rush you, little one. I make space for you to dream and become whoever you are.
affirmation.hi: Main tumhe jaldi nahi karaungi, nanhe. Main tumhare sapne dekhne aur jo bhi tum ho banne ke liye jagah banaati hoon.

about.en: Garbh Sanskar affirmations are gentle, spiritually-rooted sentences a mother shares with her baby. The calm and love behind your words begin shaping a peaceful inner world for you both, long before your baby is born.
about.hi: Garbh Sanskar affirmations woh pyaari, aadhyatmik vaakya hain jo ek maa apne baby se kehti hai. Aapke shabdon ke peeche ki shaanti aur pyaar, baby ke janm se bahut pehle hi, aap dono ke liye ek shaant andaruni duniya banane lagte hain.

howToUse.en: Sit quietly, rest a hand on your belly, and read the affirmation slowly — once silently, once aloud. Let yourself believe it for these few moments. A minute is enough, and repeating it at the same time each day makes it a calming ritual.
howToUse.hi: Shaanti se baithein, ek haath pet par rakhein, aur affirmation dheere se padhein — ek baar mann mein, ek baar bolkar. In kuch palon ke liye use sach maan lein. Ek minute kaafi hai, aur ise har din ek hi samay dohraana ise ek shaant ritual bana deta hai.

title.en: I Can Picture Good Things Coming
title.hi: Main Achhi Cheezein Aati Hui Dekh Sakti Hoon

content.en: I will let myself imagine warm, happy moments ahead with my baby. Picturing joy is a way of inviting it.
content.hi: Main khud ko apne baby ke saath aage ke garm, khush pal kalpana karne dungi. Khushi ki tasveer banana use bulaane ka ek tareeka hai.

remember.en: What you imagine with love, you draw a little closer.
remember.hi: Jo aap pyaar se kalpana karti hain, use thoda paas le aati hain.

babyLearning.en: soft and curled, growing rounder each day
babyLearning.hi: naram aur simta hua, har din aur gol hota hua

insight.en: Every story you tell your child gives them a new room to walk through inside their own mind.
insight.hi: Har kahani jo aap apne bachche ko sunaati hain unhe apne hi mann ke andar chalne ke liye ek naya kamra deti hai.

expanded.en: Stories are not just entertainment; they are how a child first travels beyond their own small world. Through a tale, a little one becomes brave, kind, frightened and triumphant — all from the safe warmth of your lap. Each story stretches their sense of what is possible and quietly teaches them how people feel and why. The more stories a child hears, the richer the inner world they carry, and the more language and empathy they grow. When you tell your child a story, you are handing them a whole world to keep, one they can return to whenever they wish.
expanded.hi: Kahaniyaan sirf manoranjan nahi hain; yahi woh tareeka hai jisse ek bachcha pehli baar apni chhoti duniya se aage safar karta hai. Ek kahani ke zariye, ek nanha bahaadur, dayaalu, dara hua aur vijayi ban jaata hai — sab aapki god ki surakshit garmi se. Har kahani unke is ehsaas ko phailaati hai ki kya mumkin hai aur chupchaap sikhaati hai ki log kaise mehsoos karte hain aur kyun. Ek bachcha jitni zyada kahaniyaan sunta hai, utni hi ameer andaruni duniya woh saath rakhta hai, aur utni hi zyada bhaasha aur empathy badhata hai. Jab aap apne bachche ko ek kahani sunaati hain, aap unhe rakhne ke liye ek poori duniya thama rahi hain, jismein woh jab chahein laut sakte hain.

deepDive.en: Studies on shared storytelling show it builds vocabulary, narrative thinking and empathy — children who hear many stories better understand that others have feelings and minds of their own.
deepDive.hi: Saath kahani sunaane par hui studies dikhaati hain ki yeh shabd-bhandaar, kahani sochne ki shakti aur empathy banaati hai — jo bachche bahut kahaniyaan sunte hain woh behtar samajhte hain ki doosron ki apni bhaavnaayein aur soch hoti hai.

remember.en: A story is a world you can give a child to keep.
remember.hi: Ek kahani ek duniya hai jo aap ek bachche ko rakhne ke liye de sakti hain.

summary.en: A kind, poor girl is given a magic brush, and learns that what she paints, she should give away with love.
summary.hi: Ek dayaalu, gareeb ladki ko ek jaadui brush milta hai, aur woh seekhti hai ki jo woh banaaye, use pyaar se baant de.

body.en:
Long ago there lived a poor but kind-hearted girl named Mei, who loved to draw though she had no brush of her own. She drew in the dust with a stick, dreaming of colours. One night an old woman appeared in her dreams and gave her a fine paintbrush. 'Use it with a good heart,' the old woman said, 'and whatever you paint will come to life.'

When Mei woke, the brush lay in her hand. She painted a bird — and it flew off the paper, singing. She painted rice for the hungry, blankets for the cold, and a sturdy ox to help the farmers plough. Whatever her village needed, she painted and gave freely.

A greedy lord heard of the brush and seized it, painting heaps of gold for himself. But in his selfish hands the gold turned to dust, for the brush's magic worked only for a giving heart. Mei got her brush back, and used it, as always, to paint good things for others — and the magic never failed her again.
body.hi:
Bahut samay pehle ek gareeb par dayaalu dil wali ladki rehti thi jiska naam Mei tha, jise chitra banana bahut pasand tha halaanki uske paas apna koi brush nahi tha. Woh ek lakdi se dhool mein banaati, rangon ke sapne dekhti. Ek raat ek boodhi aurat uske sapne mein aayi aur use ek achha paintbrush diya. 'Ise achhe dil se istemaal karna,' boodhi aurat ne kaha, 'aur jo bhi tum banaaogi woh zinda ho jaayega.'

Jab Mei jaagi, brush uske haath mein tha. Usne ek chidiya banayi — aur woh kaagaz se ud gayi, gaati hui. Usne bhookhon ke liye chawal banaaye, thandi ke maaron ke liye kambal, aur kisaanon ki hal chalaane mein madad ke liye ek mazboot bail. Uske gaon ko jo bhi chahiye, woh banaati aur khushi se de deti.

Ek laalchi raja ne brush ke baare mein suna aur use chheen liya, apne liye sone ke dher banaate hue. Par uske swaarthi haathon mein sona dhool ban gaya, kyunki brush ka jaadu sirf ek dene wale dil ke liye kaam karta tha. Mei ko apna brush wapas mila, aur usne use, hamesha ki tarah, doosron ke liye achhi cheezein banane mein istemaal kiya — aur jaadu ne use phir kabhi nakaara nahi.

title.en: Tell your baby about the little world you'll build together in play.
title.hi: Apne baby ko us nanhi duniya ke baare mein bataayein jo aap khel mein saath banaayengi.

motivation.en: The make-believe worlds you'll share are some of childhood's truest treasures.
motivation.hi: Jo make-believe duniya aap baantengi woh bachpan ke sabse sachhe khazaanon mein se hain.

description.en: Late-night raga for deep, tender stillness
description.hi: Der raat ka raag, gehri, komal sthirta ke liye

affirmation.en: As this late-night melody drifts, a deep, tender stillness settles in me, for you.
affirmation.hi: Jaise yeh der raat ki dhun behti hai, ek gehri, komal sthirta mujhmein basti hai, tumhare liye.

about.en: Ragas are classical Indian melodies, each tied to a mood and time of day. From about week 18 your baby can hear; even now, the calm a raga brings you surrounds your baby and gently shapes a peaceful environment around your pregnancy.
about.hi: Raag classical Bharatiya dhunein hain, har ek kisi mood aur din ke samay se judi. Lagbhag 18ve hafte se aapka baby sun sakta hai; abhi bhi, raag se aane wali shaanti aapke baby ko gher leti hai aur aapki pregnancy ke around ek shaant mahaul banati hai.

howToUse.en: Play the raga softly while you sit or rest with a hand on your belly. You needn't focus hard — let the sound fill the room as you breathe slowly. Two to five minutes, ideally at the raga's time of day, makes it a ritual your baby will one day recognise.
howToUse.hi: Raag dheere se chalaayein jab aap baithein ya pet par haath rakh kar aaram karein. Zyada dhyan lagane ki zaroorat nahi — aawaz ko kamre mein bhar jaane dein jab aap dheere saans lein. Do se paanch minute, ho sake to raag ke samay par, ise ek aisa ritual bana deta hai jise aapka baby ek din pehchaanega.

content.en: If you feel more tired or pale than usual, your iron may be running low as your baby grows. Try adding iron-rich foods like leafy greens, lentils and jaggery, and pair them with a little vitamin C, like lemon or amla, to help your body absorb more.
content.hi: Agar aap aam se zyada thaki ya peeli mehsoos karti hain, to ho sakta hai aapka iron kam ho raha ho jaise aapka baby badhta hai. Iron se bharpoor cheezein jaise hathe saag, daal aur gud jodne ki koshish karein, aur unke saath thoda vitamin C, jaise neembu ya amla, milaayein taaki aapka sharir zyada absorb kare.

remember.en: A little vitamin C helps iron do its quiet work.
remember.hi: Thoda vitamin C iron ko uska shaant kaam karne mein madad karta hai.

babyLearning.en: closing the week, dreaming in your warmth
babyLearning.hi: hafta poora karte hue, aapki garmi mein sapne dekhte hue

insight.en: When a child says the floor is lava or the chair is a horse, the kindest thing you can do is to play along.
insight.hi: Jab ek bachcha kahe ki farsh lava hai ya kursi ek ghoda hai, sabse dayaalu cheez jo aap kar sakti hain woh hai saath khelna.

expanded.en: It is tempting to correct a child — to say 'that is only a chair' or 'there is no dragon.' But when you step into their make-believe instead, something lovely happens. You tell your child that their inner world matters, that their imagination is welcome, that you take their wonder seriously. You do not have to be a brilliant actor; you only have to say 'yes, and' instead of 'no, but.' Playing along costs you a few minutes and gives your child something lasting: the deep, quiet sense that the worlds they dream up are worth sharing with the people they love.
expanded.hi: Ek bachche ko sahi karna sahaj lagta hai — yeh kehna ki 'woh to sirf ek kursi hai' ya 'koi dragon nahi hai.' Par jab aap iske bajaaye unke make-believe mein kadam rakhti hain, kuch pyaara hota hai. Aap apne bachche ko batati hain ki unki andaruni duniya maayne rakhti hai, ki unki kalpana ka swagat hai, ki aap unke vismay ko gambheerta se leti hain. Aapko ek shaandaar actor hone ki zaroorat nahi; aapko bas 'na, par' ke bajaaye 'haan, aur' kehna hai. Saath khelna aapke kuch minute leta hai aur aapke bachche ko kuch sthayi deta hai: woh gehra, shaant ehsaas ki jo duniya woh sapne mein banaate hain woh un logon ke saath baantne layak hai jinhe woh pyaar karte hain.

deepDive.en: When a caregiver joins a child's pretend play and follows their lead, it deepens both the bond and the play itself — children whose imaginative worlds are welcomed tend to play more richly and feel more secure.
deepDive.hi: Jab ek caregiver ek bachche ke pretend play mein judta hai aur unki agvaani follow karta hai, yeh bond aur khel dono ko gehra karta hai — jin bachchon ki kalpanaaon ki duniya ka swagat hota hai woh zyada richly khelte hain aur zyada surakshit mehsoos karte hain.

remember.en: Say 'yes, and' to the worlds your child dreams up.
remember.hi: Jo duniya aapka bachcha sapne mein banaaye usse 'haan, aur' kahein.

summary.en: Two friends lie in the grass and discover that the same clouds can hold a hundred different worlds.
summary.hi: Do dost ghaas mein letein aur paate hain ki wahi baadal sau alag duniya thaam sakte hain.

body.en:
On a slow summer afternoon, two young friends lay back in the soft grass and looked up at the great white clouds drifting across the sky. 'That one,' said the first, pointing, 'is a galloping horse with its mane flying.' The second tilted her head. 'No,' she laughed, 'that is a ship with tall white sails, sailing across the blue.'

They looked again. The first saw a sleeping giant; the second saw a mountain of soft snow. One spotted a dragon breathing smoke; the other, a grandmother with billowing hair. The very same cloud became a dozen different things, depending on the eyes that watched it.

As the clouds slowly changed shape and floated on, the two friends realised something wonderful. The sky had given them nothing but plain white clouds — and from those, their own minds had made an entire afternoon of marvels. The world, they understood, is partly made by how we choose to see it.
body.hi:
Ek dheemi garmi ki dopahar, do jawaan dost naram ghaas mein letein aur aasmaan mein behte bade safed baadlon ko dekha. 'Woh waala,' pehle ne ishaara karte kaha, 'apni ayaal udaata ek sarpat ghoda hai.' Doosri ne sir tedha kiya. 'Nahi,' woh hansi, 'woh lambe safed paalon waala ek jahaaz hai, neele mein safar karta.'

Unhone phir dekha. Pehle ne ek sote raakshas ko dekha; doosri ne naram barf ka ek pahaad. Ek ne dhuaan chhodta ek dragon dekha; doosre ne lehraate baalon waali ek daadi. Wahi ek baadal ek darjan alag cheezein ban gaya, us par nazar daalti aankhon par nirbhar.

Jaise baadal dheere se aakaar badalte aur aage behte gaye, dono doston ko kuch adbhut samajh aaya. Aasmaan ne unhe saade safed baadlon ke siva kuch nahi diya tha — aur unse, unke apne mann ne ek poori dopahar ajoobon ki bana di. Duniya, woh samjhe, kuch hadd tak isse banti hai ki hum use kaise dekhna chunte hain.

title.en: Tell your baby about a daydream you have about them.
title.hi: Apne baby ko ek aise khwaab ke baare mein bataayein jo aap unke baare mein dekhti hain.

motivation.en: Sharing a daydream aloud is a way of letting your baby into your heart.
motivation.hi: Ek khwaab ko bolkar baantna apne baby ko apne dil mein aane dene ka ek tareeka hai.

description.en: A short meditation to soften and let go
description.hi: Naram hone aur chhod dene ka ek chhota dhyan

affirmation.en: I let my thoughts drift by like soft clouds, and rest gently here with you.
affirmation.hi: Main apne vichaaron ko naram baadlon ki tarah behne deti hoon, aur yahaan tumhare saath pyaar se aaram karti hoon.

introduction.en: Sit or lie back comfortably and close your eyes. Take a few slow breaths. Imagine you are watching soft white clouds drift slowly across a wide blue sky, and let each thought float past like a cloud, asking nothing of you.
introduction.hi: Aaram se baithein ya letein aur aankhein band karein. Kuch dheemi saansein lein. Sochein ki aap ek chaude neele aasmaan mein dheere behte naram safed baadlon ko dekh rahi hain, aur har vichaar ko ek baadal ki tarah behne dein, aapse kuch maange bina.

closingThought.en: Your mind is a little lighter now, and your baby rested in that quiet with you.
closingThought.hi: Aapka mann ab thoda halka hai, aur aapka baby us shaanti mein aapke saath aaraam karta raha.

about.en: Garbh Sanskar meditations are short, gentle practices to connect with your baby — not generic mindfulness, but a few minutes of sending warmth and love inward. Your calm becomes your baby's calm.
about.hi: Garbh Sanskar meditations chhote, pyaare abhyaas hain apne baby se judne ke liye — koi aam mindfulness nahi, balki kuch minute garmjoshi aur pyaar andar bhejne ke. Aapki shaanti aapke baby ki shaanti ban jaati hai.

howToUse.en: Find a quiet spot, sit comfortably and close your eyes. Follow the introduction, breathe slowly, and rest your attention gently on your baby. Two to three minutes is plenty. Doing it daily, ideally at the same hour, deepens the bond.
howToUse.hi: Ek shaant jagah chunein, aaram se baithein aur aankhein band karein. Introduction follow karein, dheere se saans lein, aur apna dhyan pyaar se apne baby par tikaayein. Do se teen minute kaafi hai. Ise rozana, ho sake to ek hi samay karne se bond gehra hota hai.

content.en: Close your eyes and simply watch your breath as if it were a cloud — in, and out, with no effort to change it. Follow five slow breaths drifting by.
content.hi: Aankhein band karein aur bas apni saans ko aise dekhein jaise woh ek baadal ho — andar, aur baahar, use badalne ki koshish kiye bina. Paanch dheemi saanson ko behte hue follow karein.

remember.en: Watching the breath float is rest enough for the mind.
remember.hi: Saans ko behte dekhna mann ke liye kaafi aaram hai.
